spec.reasoning: The instruction explicitly references columns by letter (B, D, E, F), so inserting a title row shifts the header from row 1 to row 2 and all data rows down by one, testing whether the model hardcodes row numbers. The answer position F2:F6 already accounts for the original layout; after the title row insert it becomes F3:F7 — but since we are only perturbing the spreadsheet (not the answer), the model must adapt. I did NOT rename any columns because the instruction refers to columns B, D, E, F by letter rather than by header name, and several header names like 'REF', 'STATUS', 'SUBREFS' closely echo words used semantically in the instruction ('reference', 'subreferences'), making renaming risky per Rule 6. A distractor column 'SUBCOUNT' is added after the last column — it is plausibly similar to 'No. Subs' / 'Subs?' but clearly different, testing whether the model confuses it with the real target columns.
